hypothesis: Chemical foundation models pre-trained on large SMILES corpora can generate molecular embeddings that, when aggregated through composition-weighted schemes and combined with device-level variables, enable accurate predictive transfer learning across multiple scales of battery material properties—from molecular quantum chemistry to device performance—outperforming traditional Morgan Fingerprint baselines, because the rich chemical representations encoded during pre-training capture generalizable structure-property relationships that persist across hierarchical scales of battery system complexity.
